en.extract:
The Atlas pied flycatcher or Atlas flycatcher (Ficedula speculigera) is a bird in an Old World flycatcher family, one of the four species of Western Palearctic black-and-white flycatchers; it is endemic as a breeding species to North-west Africa.
It was formerly regarded as a race of European pied flycatcher, but Sætre et al. (2001) recommended that it is regarded as a species in its own right. The bird has large white patches on its wing and forehead. Identification is also covered in Etherington and Small (2003) and van den Berg et al. (2006).


References


External links
"Ficedula speculigera". Avibase.

[[File:Ficedula speculigera MHNT.ZOO.2010.11.206.4.jpg|thumb|''Ficedula speculigera'' - [[MHNT]]]]
The '''Atlas pied flycatcher''' or '''Atlas flycatcher''' ('''''Ficedula speculigera''''') is a [[bird]] in an [[Old World flycatcher]] family, one of the four species of [[Western Palearctic black-and-white flycatchers]]; it is [[Endemism in birds|endemic]] as a breeding species to [[North-west Africa]].<ref name=":0">{{Cite web|url=https://ebird.org/species/atlfly1|title=Atlas Flycatcher - eBird|website=ebird.org|language=en|access-date=2020-03-07}}</ref>

It was formerly regarded as a race of [[European pied flycatcher]], but Sætre ''et al.'' (2001) recommended that it is regarded as a species in its own right.<ref name="Saetre et al., 2001"/> The bird has large white patches on its wing and forehead.<ref name=":0" /> Identification is also covered in Etherington and Small (2003)<ref name="Etherington et al., 2003"/> and van den Berg ''et al.'' (2006).<ref name="van den Berg et al., 2006"/>
